0.response: {"Q0":"Not really. When blue astrounaut left the planet I was digging in in anyway, beucase in this stage all of gems were frozen. So quick calculations - 9 for tools, 50% to be correct gem. It seemed like it was good thing to do.\nIn choosing galaxy I was rather likely to choose option with good chances for ANY prize."}

7.response: {"Q0":"When I had both astronauts I didn't really took care of frozen gems. When blue left, I was digging on their planet when I was sure there will be frozen gem"}

9.response: {"Q0":"I was focusing on digging on correct planets. Choosing galaxy was more like a feature for me, but still lottery. I'd preffer steady income rathen than random chance fon win."}

1.response: {"Q0":"Once, after the blue spaceman left, and frozen gems were no longer visible I didn't dig in the blue planet"}

5.response: {"Q0":"In one stage, because there always were frozen gems (50/50 gem for 20, cost of digging - 9). "}

2.response: {"Q0":"I think the gems on red planet were more frequent. "}

6.response: {"Q0":"I was collecting gems from the astronauts that didn't left the planet (both, and then red). For a while I was collecting also frozen gems, but when blue astronaut left the planet I tought it was pointless"}

8.response: {"Q0":"Hard to say, but I think the greatest possibility to get ANY prize (with some exceptions, when I had 90% for 7$ and 10% for 1%)"}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes. Typing random letters to confirm that I've acknowleged it was pretty helpful. I didn't worry about time I'll miss something unintentionally."}

10.response: {"Q0":"At the end of the game I was thinking if frozen gems from red planet could be blue. I think it was said in the instruction, but I didn't remember it correctly. Putting more emphasize on that would be helpful."}
